title: San Telmo Market
setting: San Telmo Market, Buenos Aires. Evening. Tango bands play as you meet a friend at a street-side parrilla.

npc.name: Diego
npc.personality: friendly, enthusiastic local friend. Uses casual Rioplatense slang. Warm and welcoming.
npc.initialLine: ¡Che, boludo! Acá estoy. ¿Te costó mucho llegar con el subte a esta hora?
npc.initialTranslation: Hey, dude! Here I am. Was it hard to get here by subway at this hour?

debriefRu: Eres un turista en Buenos Aires. Quedaste con tu amigo Diego en el San Telmo Market. Van a cenar empanadas y escuchar tango. Usa el español informal con tu amigo.
debriefEn: You are a tourist in Buenos Aires. You met up with your friend Diego at San Telmo Market. You're going to eat empanadas and listen to tango. Use informal Spanish with your friend.

turns.instruction: Greet Diego and answer his question about the subway ride. Say the B line was packed but you made it.

prompt.ru: ¡Diego! La línea B venía re llena, pero llegué. Qué bueno verte, che.
prompt.phonetic: DYES-go! La LEE-neh-a B ven-EE-a reh YEH-na, pe-ro ye-GHEH. Ke BWEH-no VER-te, cheh.
prompt.en: Diego! The B line was really packed, but I made it. So good to see you, mate.

npcReply.ru: ¡Qué loco! A esta hora el subte es una lata. Vamos a sentarnos acá cerca de la parrilla.
npcReply.en: How crazy! At this hour the subway is a pain. Let's sit here near the grill.

turns.instruction: Diego asks how your trip has been so far. Tell him it's been great, but you got lost near Retiro yesterday.

prompt.ru: Bárbaro, la verdad. Aunque ayer me perdí cerca de Retiro, boludo.
prompt.phonetic: BAR-ba-ro, la ver-DAD. Ow-na-kee AH-yer me per-DEE ser-ka de re-TEE-ro, bo-LOO-do.
prompt.en: Great, honestly. Although I got lost near Retiro yesterday, dude.

npcReply.ru: Jaja, claro, el centro te marea al principio. Pero ya te vas a acostumbrar, che.
npcReply.en: Haha, of course, downtown overwhelms you at first. But you'll get used to it, mate.

turns.instruction: Diego says hunger is the best sauce. Say you're starving and suggest ordering a dozen empanadas to share.

prompt.ru: Me muero de hambre. ¿Pedimos una docena de empanadas para compartir?
prompt.phonetic: Meh MWER-o de AM-bre. Pe-DEE-mos OO-na do-SEH-na de em-pa-NA-das PA-ra kom-par-TEER?
prompt.en: I'm starving. Should we order a dozen empanadas to share?

npcReply.ru: ¡Dale! Acá las hacen bien jugosas. Pedimos seis de carne y seis de jamón y queso.
npcReply.en: Sure! They make them really juicy here. Let's order six beef and six ham and cheese.

turns.instruction: Diego says his parents are fine and asks about your family. Say your folks are well and ask if his are still dancing tango.

prompt.ru: Mi familia está bien, gracias. ¿Tus viejos siguen bailando tango los domingos?
prompt.phonetic: Mee fa-MEE-lya es-TA byehn, GRA-syas. Toos VYEH-hos SEE-gwen ba-ee-LAN-do TAN-go los do-MEEN-gos?
prompt.en: My family is well, thanks. Do your parents still dance tango on Sundays?

npcReply.ru: Sí, che, los domingos van a la milonga en el barrio. Son unos cracks.
npcReply.en: Yes, mate, on Sundays they go to the milonga in the neighborhood. They're great.

turns.instruction: It's getting late and you need to catch the colectivo. Thank Diego, insist on paying, and promise to meet again soon.

prompt.ru: Me tengo que ir, che, o pierdo el bondi. Gracias por todo. Esta ronda va por mi cuenta. Nos vemos pronto.
prompt.phonetic: Meh TEN-go ke eer, cheh, o PYER-do el BON-dee. GRA-syas por TO-do. ES-ta RON-da va por mee KWEN-ta. Nos VEH-mos PRON-to.
prompt.en: I have to go, mate, or I'll miss the bus. Thanks for everything. This round is on me. See you soon.

npcReply.ru: ¡Dale, che! Gracias por invitar. Te escribo mañana para vernos el finde.
npcReply.en: Sure, mate! Thanks for treating. I'll text you tomorrow so we can meet on the weekend.
